creator: Alexander Terp
description:
RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-14 (rad v0.10.1): still reproduces.

`and` always returns a boolean, but `or` returns the actual operand value (short-circuit semantics). These are inconsistent.

**Repro:**
print(1 and 2)   // true  (boolean)
print(1 or 2)    // 1     (the operand value)
print(0 or 2)    // 2     (the operand value)

**Source:** `core/expr_ops.go:61-75` (and returns bool; or returns the operand).

Users from Python/JS expect both to return operand values (short-circuit), or both to return booleans.

**Design question:** unify the two - and changing either is a potentially breaking semantic change.
title: and returns bool while or returns operand value - inconsistent
